title: Flit
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Flit.png]] |name=Flit |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]]{{GalaxiesAED}} |class=[[Reptile/Legends|Reptile]]''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]'' |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] and green |eyecolor= |distinctions=[[Poison/Legends|Poisonous]] barbs[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] |lifespan= |origin=[[Lok/Legends|Lok]] |habitat= |diet=Hematophagous{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=holocron|The Galaxy's Inhabitants - Lok}} |language= }} {{Quote|The only chance the malkloc has is the fact that the flit has a damaged wing from its tussle with the dire cat in the previous round.''"
"''And the fact that they normally hunt in swarms.|[[Tendau Nandon]] and [[Dusque Mistflier]]|}} '''Flits''' were [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] flying [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lok/Legends|Lok]]. They had thick hides and extremely sharp beaks as well as a number of [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] barbs, making them a dangerous predator. Flits were also found on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell's]] [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]] and the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] world [[Despayre]]. On Lok, the [[Reptavian/Legends|reptavians]] were [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] and used for meat, but they could also be tamed and made into effective fighters. One such tamed flit won the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] sport competition at the [[Aerie Casino opening|opening]] of the [[Aerie Casino]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. ==Biology and appearance== Flits were [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]], [[Reptavian/Legends|flying reptiles]] usually growing a wingspan greater than the height of a tall [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]]. They had two smaller [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] with sharp claws for grabbing. Flits had thick hides, very sharp beaks—[[Color/Legends|orange]] at the peak but fading in color toward the head—and a small tuft on the back of their neck. Their plumage was a mix of blue and green shades. They also had dorsal barbs tipped with a powerful [[Poison/Legends|poison]]. A small prick from the barbs was capable of felling a grown [[Human/Legends|Human]]. There was some variety within the flit [[Species/Legends|species]], such as flit harassers, flit bloodsuckers, and giant flits. These differed in strength and size.[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] Flits attacked in swarms, swooping around their prey, alternately advancing and retreating. They were particularly effective attacking at night, when it was much harder to avoid them. Despite their savagery, flits could be tamed. ==History== [[File:Flits.png|thumb|left|180px|Flits interacting in their native environment]] Flits were indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lok/Legends|Lok]], located in the [[Karthakk sector/Legends|Karthakk sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. There, they were found around the barren land surrounding [[Nym's Stronghold|the stronghold]] of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Despite the danger posed by the flits, the locals hunted their meat and used their [[Bone/Legends|bones]].In ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', players can receive "Lokian Avian Meat" and "Lokian Avian Bones" upon killing flits. Elsewhere in the Mid Rim,''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' flits were also found aboard [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], which [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|A Small Hunting Trip|Tansarii Point Station}} of the [[Bright Jewel Oversector/Legends|Bright Jewel Oversector]].''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' The [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] hunter [[Wyle Artis]] sought to hire a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to hunt some flits and [[Scyk/Legends|scyks]] for him in the Tansarii Point atrium, offering [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and a necklace for successfully completing the job. Flits were also found on [[Despayre]], located in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] They occasionally infiltrated the perimeter fences of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] compounds stationed planetside, causing a threat to the residents there. All the flits on Despayre perished with the [[Destruction of Despayre|destruction]] of the planet by the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] that occurred in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], a trained flit harasser was entered into the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] sport competition that marked the [[Aerie Casino opening|opening]] of the [[Aerie Casino]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Trained by a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]], the reptavian eventually won the competition, defeating a [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomiri]] [[malkloc]] in the final round by landing on its back and stabbing its sharp beak deep into the malkloc's neck. The [[Laser Flits]], a gang from [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], was named after the creature.{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Wald's Woes|Tatooine}} ==Behind the scenes== Flits were created for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and produced by [[LucasArts]], that debuted in [[2003]] and shut down on [[December 15]], [[2011]].{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}} In the game, they were an opponent on Lok, one of the ten original playable worlds, as well as part of a quest for beginner players on [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]]. ==Appearances== *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]'' *{{GalaxiesAED}} {{1st}} *{{GalaxiesNGE}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided Quick Reference Guide]]'' *[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{SWGWiki}} [[Category:Creatures of Lok]] [[Category:Flying creatures]] [[Category:Herd and pack creatures]] [[Category:Non-sentient species]] [[Category:Predatory creatures]] [[Category:Reptavians]] [[Category:Venomous and poisonous creatures]]
